verdict_reasoning: BAX is a pro-apoptotic BCL-2 family member normally resident in the cytoplasm and outer mitochondrial membrane (OMM). Under apoptotic stress, BAX undergoes conformational change, oligomerizes at the OMM, and forms a pore. During this apoptotic state, BAX can also transiently expose epitopes accessible to antibodies and other binders from outside the cell. Additionally, in some contexts (notably ICD / immunogenic cell death), apoptotic cells exhibit surface exposure of BAX and other OMM proteins through loss of membrane integrity and PM blebbing. BAX is not a baseline plasma membrane protein, but becomes surface-accessible during apoptosis-driven cell death when OMM becomes continuous with PM or during secondary necrosis. This qualifies as contextual rather than baseline surface accessibility.
